title: Importing Subitems
excerpt:
Hi team, 
Is it possible to import subitems to an exisiting board? 
I created a CRM and had ‘people’ linked to the ‘companies’ board - but is this best to do as a subitem? 
Thoughts please. 
Thanks!

posts.author: Courtenay (Courtenay)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi team,

Is it possible to import subitems to an exisiting board?
I created a CRM and had ‘people’ linked to the ‘companies’ board - but is this best to do as a subitem?

Thoughts please.

Thanks!

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Courtenay

Subitems can be somewhat limited in different ways, such as with automations (not too many subitem supporting recipes available) and with import. There isn’t currently an easy upload for subitems into existing items on a board.

If you wanted to have a chat around overall approach and subitems or the workflow requirements in general, I’ve got great availability this week and next, just let me know

Peta | upstream
peta@upstreamtech.io
+613 9067 5611

posts.author: Courtenay (Courtenay)
posts.cookedHtml: That’d be great Peta, do you have availability today?

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Courtenay,

If you’re still looking for a solution on this, you can now import your data into subitems using the Data Importer monday app.

excels monday sub items1641×875 116 KB

I’ve also made a tutorial for the step-by-step on how to do this with Excel.

Happy to answer any questions on this or take feedback requests!

James

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
In a follow up to the above, it is now also possible to use Data Importer to import subitems into multiple items in the same import:

dataimporter.co

How to import subitems into multiple monday items from Excel (Integration)

Import subitems from Excel into multiple monday subitems.
